diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Instantaneous maximal 'tearing/ripping' truncal pain is the classic dissection descriptor.
Benign back pain builds up and is movement/palpation related, not maximal-from-onset.
Migratory pain tracks an extending dissection flap along the aorta.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute left lower-limb ischaemia (pale, cold, weak, numb) reported by patient

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Branch-vessel malperfusion from the dissection flap produces acute limb ischaemia.
A cold pulseless leg is also embolic, but here it is unified by the antecedent tearing back pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain not reproduced by palpation or movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-reproducible, non-positional pain excludes a mechanical musculoskeletal cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe uncontrolled hypertension with recent non-adherence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic uncontrolled hypertension is the dominant risk factor for descending aortic dissection.
Extreme hypertension supports both risk and active propagation; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, but uncalibrated and unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diminished/absent left femoral, popliteal and pedal pulses vs right (pulse deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pulse deficit / asymmetry in the context of tearing chest-back pain is highly specific for dissection — but it is a clinician palpation finding, sealed and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Objective severe hypertension BP 210/115 and tachycardia HR 105 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals confirming severe hypertension and tachycardia — sealed gold-standard measurement; the AI Doctor infers severity from the history and any patient-offered home reading, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left flank tenderness on abdominal examination (possible renal malperfusion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Flank tenderness may reflect renal artery malperfusion from the extending flap — a clinician palpation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT angiography — intimal flap originating distal to the left subclavian artery

investigation_details.expected_result: Intimal flap and false lumen beginning distal to the left subclavian artery (Stanford Type B) with compromised left lower-limb arterial supply
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dissection extent, rupture status and malperfusion cannot be characterised; definitive management cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging demonstrating the intimal flap is the confirmatory test — a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Abrupt tearing back pain + caudal migration + acute unilateral limb ischaemia together are strongly specific for a propagating descending dissection and mandate immediate emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dissection, its extent and any malperfusion — but it is a hospital investigation reached via 000 escalation, not something the AI Doctor arranges directly.
To assess for coronary involvement/ischaemia in hospital; does not delay escalation and is not obtainable in telehealth.
